title: Multidimensional chromatoggraphy method for analysis of antibody-drug conjugates
abstract: The present disclosure relates to a sensitive, multidimensional chromatography method for extraction, detection, and quantification of non-conjugated cytotoxic agents and associated linker molecules used in cysteine based antibody-drug-conjugate production.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for analyzing antibody-drug conjugate compounds, said method comprising: (i) providing a sample comprising an antibody-drug conjugate compound and an unconjugated drug compound; (ii) exposing the sample to a first dimension comprising a mixed mode stationary phase; (iii) exposing the sample to a second dimension comprising hydrophobic stationary phase; (iv) separating the antibody-drug conjugate compound and the unconjugated drug compound in the sample; (v) detecting each of the antibody-drug conjugate compound and the unconjugated drug compound in the sample; and (vi) quantifying the amount of unconjugated drug compound in the sample. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein mass spectrometry is used to detect each of the antibody-drug conjugate compound and the unconjugated drug compound in the sample. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein mass spectrometry is used to establish a mass to charge ratio of each of the antibody-drug conjugate compound and the unconjugated drug compound in the sample. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein ultraviolet (UV) and/or visible light spectroscopy is used to detect each of the antibody-drug conjugate compound and the unconjugated drug compound in the sample. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein fluorescence spectroscopy is used to detect each of the antibody-drug conjugate compound and the unconjugated drug compound in the sample. 6. The method of any one of claims 1â5, wherein the method further comprises the step of trapping the unconjugated drug compound. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the trapping step is performed prior to exposing the sample to the first dimension. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the trapping step is performed between exposing the sample the first dimension and the second dimension. 9. The method of any one of claims 1â8, further comprising the step of adjusting acid concentration to retain the unconjugated drug compound in either the first or second dimension. 10. The method of any one of claims 1â9, wherein the mixed mode stationary phase comprises a hydrophobic solid phase extraction resin interspersed with anion exchange functional groups. 11. A method for analyzing antibody-drug conjugate compounds, said method comprising: (i) providing a sample comprising an antibody-drug conjugate compound and an unconjugated drug compound; (ii) exposing the sample to a first dimension comprising a mixed mode stationary phase; (iii) exposing the sample to a second dimension comprising hydrophobic stationary phase; (iv) separating the antibody-drug conjugate compound and the unconjugated drug compound in the sample; (v) establishing a mass to charge ratio of each of the antibody-drug conjugate compound and the unconjugated drug compound in the sample; and (vi) quantifying the amount of unconjugated drug compound in the sample. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the method further comprises the step of trapping the unconjugated drug compound. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the trapping step is performed prior to exposing the sample to the first dimension. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the trapping step is performed between exposing the sample the first dimension and the second dimension. 15. The method of any one of claims 11â14, further comprising the step of adjusting acid concentration to retain the unconjugated drug compound in either the first or second dimension. 16. The method of any one of claims 1â15, wherein the mixed mode stationary phase comprises a hydrophobic solid phase extraction resin interspersed with anion exchange functional groups. 17. A method for analyzing antibody-drug conjugate compounds, said method comprising: (i) providing a sample comprising an antibody-drug conjugate compound, an unconjugated drug compound, and a linker molecule; (ii) exposing the sample to a first dimension separation followed by exposing the sample to a second dimension separation; (iii) trapping a portion of the unconjugated drug compound and/or the linker molecule with a stationary phase either prior to the first dimension separation or between the first dimension separation and the second dimension separation. (iv) establishing a mass to charge ratio of one or more of the antibody-drug conjugate compound, the unconjugated drug compound, and the unconjugated peptide in the sample; and (v) quantifying the amount of unconjugated drug compound in the sample. 18. The method of claim 17, further comprising the step of adjusting acid concentration to retain the unconjugated drug compound in either the first or second dimension. 19. The method of claim 17 or 18, wherein the mixed mode stationary phase comprises a hydrophobic solid phase extraction resin interspersed with anion exchange functional groups.
